meta.description: Performance physical therapy for mountain athletes in Denver and Boulder. Fix the root cause of your injury and get back to the trails, the rock, and the snow.

index.title: Summit Sports PT — Performance Physical Therapy in Denver & Boulder
index.description: Performance physical therapy for mountain athletes in Denver and Boulder. Get back to the trails, the rock, and the snow — stronger than before.

blocks.eyebrow: Performance Physical Therapy for Mountain Athletes in Denver & Boulder
blocks.headline: Get back to the mountains, not just out of pain
blocks.subhead: We fix the root cause of your injury so you can run the trails, climb the rock, and ski the lines that make you you.

blocks.headline: An injury takes more than your miles
blocks.body: When pain keeps you off the trail, off the wall, or off the slopes, it takes more than your fitness. It takes the part of you that comes alive outside. Generic stretches and rest-and-pray won't bring it back.

blocks.headline: We care about your injury as much as you do
blocks.tagline: We chase summits too — and we know how hard it is when you can't.

steps.title: Book a FREE discovery call
steps.body: Hop on a quick 20-minute call so we can understand your goals, your injury, and whether we're the right fit.

steps.title: Get matched with your guide
steps.body: We pair you with the therapist who specializes in exactly what you need and build a plan around your life.

steps.title: Return to the mountains — better
steps.body: Execute the plan with a coach in your corner and come back stronger than before, without fear of re-injury.

blocks.headline: You lose a piece of yourself when the mountains are off the table
blocks.body: Performance physical therapy doesn't just quiet the pain — it eliminates it at the source, so you can keep pushing without holding back.

items.title: Performance Physical Therapy
items.body: We don't slap a band-aid on the sore spot — we fix the root cause. 60-minute one-on-one sessions combining manual therapy, dry needling, and real strength work.

items.title: Data-Driven Care
items.body: Running gait analysis, strength testing, and blood flow restriction — objective tools that find what's actually going on instead of guessing.

items.title: Built for Your Sport
items.body: Trail runner, climber, skier — your plan is built around the demands of your sport and the lines you want to get back to.

blocks.headline: We've helped Front Range athletes get back to what they love
blocks.body: It starts with a free discovery call and a customized plan. From there, you execute with us in your corner — follow-up after every session, a clear weekly progression, and a clinician who actually answers when you reach out.
items: Move pain-free on the trail and the wall again
Perform better than you did before your injury
Stop letting fear of re-injury hold you back

blocks.headline: See what we're all about
blocks.body: A clinic built for performance and a team that treats your goals like their own. Let's find out if we're the right fit.

blocks.headline: Rolled an ankle on the trail one too many times?
blocks.body: Most advice tells you to rest and tape it up. Our free Trail-Ready Ankle Guide shows you the approach that actually builds ankle resilience for the descents — no strings.

physical-therapy.title: Physical Therapy — Summit Sports PT
physical-therapy.description: Performance physical therapy in Denver & Boulder: 60-minute one-on-one sessions that fix the root cause of your pain.

blocks.headline: We fix the root cause — not just the symptom
blocks.subhead: Every session is 60 minutes, one-on-one with your therapist. No double-booking, no handing you off to a tech.

blocks.headline: What makes performance PT different
body: Most clinics treat the spot that hurts and send you on your way. We look at how your whole body moves, find why the pain started, and build the strength and mechanics that keep it from coming back.
That means more time with you, sharper assessments, and a plan built around the trails, routes, and lines you actually want to get back to.

items.title: Hands-on manual therapy
items.body: Targeted soft-tissue and joint work to restore mobility and calm down painful tissue fast.

items.title: Dry needling
items.body: Precise relief for stubborn muscular pain and trigger points that won't let go.

items.title: Strength & conditioning
items.body: Real gym-based work that rebuilds capacity so your fix holds up under load and altitude.

items.title: Running gait analysis
items.body: Slow-motion video and metrics to find the mechanics driving your running injury.

items.title: Blood flow restriction
items.body: Build strength with lighter loads when you're early in recovery or rehabbing.

items.title: Return-to-sport testing
items.body: Objective benchmarks so you know you're ready for the trail, the wall, or the season.

steps.title: Assess
steps.body: A full-body evaluation to find the true source of your pain — not just where you feel it.

steps.title: Treat
steps.body: Hands-on care plus a personalized plan you can actually fit into your week.

steps.title: Build
steps.body: Progressive strength work so you come back more durable than you were before.

blocks.headline: What we treat every day
blocks.body: If pain is keeping you from the activities you love, there's a good chance we can help. A few of the most common reasons mountain athletes come to Summit:
items: Running & trail-running injuries (runner's knee, IT band, shin splints)

blocks.headline: Not sure if we're the right fit?
blocks.body: That's exactly what the free 20-minute call is for. No pressure — just answers.

about.title: About Us — Summit Sports PT
about.description: Meet the Summit Sports PT team — performance physical therapists who chase summits too.

blocks.headline: We chase summits too
blocks.subhead: We're runners, climbers, and skiers first — and we know exactly how it feels when an injury takes the mountains away.

blocks.headline: Our story
body: Summit started with a simple frustration: traditional physical therapy wasn't built for people who push their bodies in the mountains. Fifteen-minute visits, hot packs, and a generic sheet of exercises don't cut it when your goal is a 50K, a hard send, or a full ski season.
Founded by a former collegiate runner, we built something different — a clinic where every session is an hour, one-on-one, focused on getting you back to performing, not just pain-free on paper.

items.title: Root cause, always
items.body: We never settle for masking symptoms. We find why it started and fix it for good.

items.title: Performance, not just recovery
items.body: Our goal isn't to get you to zero pain — it's to get you back stronger than before.

items.title: You're an athlete
items.body: However you move in the mountains, we treat you like the athlete you are and train you like one.

items.title: Founder & Performance PT
items.body: Former collegiate runner who leads every new client's first discovery call.

items.title: Running Specialist
items.body: Gait analysis and load management to keep runners on the trail and out of the clinic.

items.title: Climbing & Upper-Body PT
items.body: Fingers, elbows, and shoulders — keeping climbers strong through hard projects.

items.title: Strength & Return-to-Sport
items.body: Builds the durability that gets you ready for the season, not just the next appointment.

blocks.headline: Come see what we're all about
blocks.body: Let's find out if we're the right fit — starting with a free 20-minute call.

blocks.headline: Move better, climb higher, ski longer
blocks.subhead: Practical guidance from our team on training, recovery, and staying in the game year-round.

posts.title: Why your ankles keep rolling on the trail (and what actually fixes it)
posts.excerpt: If you've sprained the same ankle more than once, the problem usually isn't bad luck. Here's how to build an ankle that holds up on technical descents.
body: If your ankle keeps rolling on rocky descents, you're not unlucky — your ankle is missing the strength and control it needs to react when the ground moves under you.
After a sprain, most people rest, tape up, and hope. But the balance, ankle strength, and reaction speed don't come back on their own. That's why the same ankle keeps giving out trip after trip.

The real fix is progressive loading, single-leg balance work on unstable surfaces, and calf and foot strength built for the demands of downhill running. That's exactly the kind of plan we build in a performance PT session — and the foundation of our free Trail-Ready Ankle Guide.

posts.title: Coming back from runner's knee without losing your season
posts.excerpt: Runner's knee doesn't have to mean months off the trail. Here's how we get Front Range runners back on the road — smartly.
body: Runner's knee is one of the most common reasons runners come to see us — and one of the most fixable.
The key is addressing the mechanics driving the pain rather than just resting until it quiets down. Rest alone tends to set you up for the same problem the moment your mileage climbs again.
With the right load management, hip and quad strength work, and small gait tweaks, most runners are back to training in weeks, not months — and back on the trails before the season slips away.

posts.title: Build a ski-ready body before the first storm
posts.excerpt: The best way to avoid a season-ending knee injury isn't luck — it's preparation. Here's where to start before opening day.
body: Most ski injuries we see aren't freak accidents — they're the result of showing up to opening day with legs that haven't been loaded since spring.

Skiing and snowboarding demand strength, control, and endurance through deep ranges your body forgets over the off-season. When fatigue sets in late in the day, that's when knees and shoulders pay the price.
A focused six-to-eight week build of single-leg strength, eccentric control, and conditioning makes a real difference. Start early, train the right way, and you'll ski stronger and longer all season.
